# Quickstart: Multiplayer Integration Scenarios
*Generated by /speckit-plan — 2026-05-15*

---

## Scenario 1: Sign In and Create a Public Lobby

```typescript
import { MultiplayerSession } from './src/multiplayer/index.js';

const session = new MultiplayerSession({
  signalingUrl: 'wss://your-server.com',
  viewer: cesiumViewer,               // Cesium.Viewer instance
});

// Open Firebase OAuth popup (Google, GitHub, Discord, or Apple)
const authResult = await session.signIn('google');
if (authResult.type !== 'ok') {
  console.error('Sign-in failed:', authResult.reason);
  return;
}
// authResult.user: { id, displayName, avatarUrl }

// Create a public lobby
const result = await session.createLobby({ isPublic: true });
if (result.type === 'connected') {
  console.log('Lobby created:', result.lobby.id);
  console.log('Join code:', result.lobby.code); // for sharing
}
```

---

## Scenario 2: Join a Private Lobby by Code

```typescript
const result = await session.joinLobby({
  lobbyId: 'abc123',
  code: 'XK9F2M',
});

switch (result.type) {
  case 'connected':
    console.log('Joined! Peers:', result.peers.map(p => p.displayName));
    break;
  case 'lobby-full':
    showError('This lobby is full (max 6 users).');
    break;
  case 'invalid-code':
    showError('Incorrect join code.');
    break;
  case 'not-found':
    showError('Lobby no longer exists.');
    break;
}
```

---

## Scenario 3: Map Annotation

The `AnnotationManager` handles all drawing tool state. Call `session.broadcastAnnotation()` when a tool action completes.

```typescript
import { AnnotationManager } from './src/multiplayer/AnnotationManager.js';

const annotations = new AnnotationManager(cesiumViewer);
annotations.onComplete = (annotation) => {
  // Broadcast to all peers via data channel
  session.broadcastAnnotation(annotation);
};

// Activate drawing tools
annotations.setTool('freehand'); // or 'line', 'circle', 'rectangle', 'pin'
annotations.setColor('#ff4444');

// Handle incoming annotations from peers
session.onAnnotation = (annotation) => {
  annotations.addRemote(annotation);
};

// Host: clear all annotations
session.clearAnnotations(); // broadcasts annotation-clear to all peers
```

---

## Scenario 4: Voice (Push-to-Talk) and Video

```typescript
// Push-to-talk: bind to a key or button
document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
  if (e.code === 'Space') session.startPTT();
});
document.addEventListener('keyup', (e) => {
  if (e.code === 'Space') session.stopPTT();
});

// Toggle video
const videoEnabled = await session.toggleVideo();
// videoEnabled: true if camera is now on, false if off

// Render remote video tracks
session.onVideoTrack = (peerId, track) => {
  const video = document.getElementById(`video-${peerId}`) as HTMLVideoElement;
  video.srcObject = new MediaStream([track]);
};
```

---

## Scenario 5: Pilot Mode

Pilot mode is initiated from a peer's avatar sprite click (handled by `PresenceAvatarManager`), but can also be called directly:

```typescript
// Follow another user's camera
const result = await session.followPeer('peer-uuid-alice');
// result: 'following' | 'peer-not-found' | 'already-following'

// While following: full annotation capability remains
annotations.setTool('pin'); // still works

// Leave pilot mode
session.leavePilotMode();
// Camera control immediately restored
```

---

## Scenario 6: Host Controls

```typescript
// Only available when session.isHost === true

// Kick a member
await session.kickPeer('peer-uuid-bob');

// Toggle lobby privacy mid-session
await session.setLobbyPublic(false);

// Lock lobby (no new joins)
await session.setLobbyLocked(true);

// Promote another member to host
await session.promoteHost('peer-uuid-carol');
// session.isHost is now false for the current client

// Broadcast current layer visibility to all peers
session.broadcastLayerState({
  fire: fireOverlay.visible,
  radio: radioMarkers.visible,
  flights: flightMarkers.visible,
});
```

---

## Scenario 7: Lobby Browser

```typescript
// Fetch current public lobbies (single request, not a subscription)
const lobbies = await session.listLobbies();
// lobbies: Array<{ id, hostDisplayName, memberCount, createdAt }>

// Render a lobby list and join on click
for (const lobby of lobbies) {
  renderLobbyCard(lobby, () => session.joinLobby({ lobbyId: lobby.id }));
}
```

---

## Scenario 8: New Joiner State Onboarding

This happens automatically — no integration required. When a new peer connects:
1. `MultiplayerSession` establishes all data channels with the new peer.
2. If `session.isHost`, it opens a `transfer-state` channel to the new peer.
3. It sends `{ type: 'state-full', annotations: [...] }` with all current annotations.
4. The new peer's `AnnotationManager` calls `addRemote()` for each annotation.
5. The transfer channel is closed after `state-ack`.

---

## Scenario 9: Cleanup

```typescript
// Leave current lobby (triggers host promotion if needed)
await session.leaveLobby();

// Fully destroy the session (call on page unload)
session.destroy();
```
